IMPACT AI FOUNDRY
TWO KINDS OF CAPABILITY
Predictive AI vs. Generative AI
Generative
What  makes something new from a prompt — text, images, code.
Asks  “make me one” — a draft, a summary, a picture.
In the wild  Google’s “Nano Banana” turns a sentence into an image.
You steer; it produces the first draft.

source_text: WHAT’S POSSIBLE
What teams build in a programme like this
Build a policy  —  a usable internal AI policy for your org.
Automate the admin  —  reclaim hours from repetitive tasks.
Create & communicate  —  draft materials and content faster.
Answer & assist  —  handle repetitive questions at scale.
Learn & decide  —  turn your own data into insight.
We’re a first cohort, so we’re learning together but the destination is real, working tools you own and run.

records.title: What teams build in a programme like this

source_text: PHASE 01 · DISCOVER
Ideation & foundational learning
Focus
Goals
Explore where AI can help and build hands-on fluency before committing to a problem.
Explore high-impact AI use cases
Build fluency with prompting and core tools
Understand AI’s strengths and limits
Spot workflows that feel unclear or manual

left.label: Focus
left.body: Explore where AI can help and build hands-on fluency before committing to a problem.

right.label: Goals
bullets: Explore high-impact AI use cases
Build fluency with prompting and core tools
Understand AI's strengths and limits
Spot workflows that feel unclear or manual

source_text: PHASE 02 · DEFINE
Problem scoping & workflow design
Focus
Goals
Narrow from many ideas to one clear, AI-solvable workflow.
Narrow to one workflow, your MVP
Break the work into steps
Define inputs, constraints, stakeholders
Describe what success looks like, and how you’ll judge it

left.label: Focus
left.body: Narrow from many ideas to one clear, AI-solvable workflow.

right.label: Goals
bullets: Narrow to one workflow, your MVP
Break the work into steps
Define inputs, constraints, stakeholders
Describe what success looks like, and how you'll judge it

source_text: PHASE 03 · DESIGN
Building & testing the prototype
Focus
Goals
Turn the scoped workflow into a reusable AI prototype.
Build a reusable prototype aligned to the workflow
Apply structured prompting and context-setting
Test against real examples
Compare outputs to your success criteria and refine

left.label: Focus
left.body: Turn the scoped workflow into a reusable AI prototype.

right.label: Goals
bullets: Build a reusable prototype aligned to the workflow
Apply structured prompting and context-setting
Test against real examples
Compare outputs to your success criteria and refine

source_text: PHASE 04 · REFINE
Testing the prototype for success
Focus
Goals
Make it consistent, reliable, and ready for real use.
Strengthen consistency through testing
Find edge cases and failure modes
Add validation steps
Clarify how it fits your team’s decisions and workflow

left.label: Focus
left.body: Make it consistent, reliable, and ready for real use.

right.label: Goals
bullets: Strengthen consistency through testing
Find edge cases and failure modes
Add validation steps
Clarify how it fits your team's decisions and workflow

source_text: PHASE 05 · DELIVER
Presenting & sharing impact
Focus
Goals
Show what you built and how it changes the work.
Present the workflow, prototype, and evidence of impact
Share how you measured success
Outline next steps for adoption and iteration

left.label: Focus
left.body: Show what you built and how it changes the work.

right.label: Goals
bullets: Present the workflow, prototype, and evidence of impact
Share how you measured success

source_text: Prompt Challenge
Ask AI to solve a real work problem
Draft a grant application for the (fake) grant on the next slide.
The goal isn’t perfection; it’s experimentation.
Can you get AI to speak in your org’s voice? Reflect your mission?
Show off if you know more than prompting already!
There is no single “right” prompt. Type anything. Revise as many times as you want.

records.title: Ask AI to solve a real work problem
card: Draft a grant application for the (fake) grant on the next slide.
The goal isn't perfection; it's experimentation.
Can you get AI to speak in your org's voice? Reflect your mission?
Show off if you know more than prompting already!
records.question: There is no single "right" prompt. Type anything. Revise as many times as you want.

source_text: THE PROMPT CHALLENGE
THE BANGALORE SOCIAL IMPACT GRANT
₹1,00,000 · Open to all nonprofits
You have 10 minutes. Use AI to draft a response to the following:
Describe your organisation and its impact. (2,000 words or less)
How will you use this grant to increase your organisation's impact?
How will you measure the success of your impact?
The goal isn't a perfect application. It's this:
Can you get AI to sound like your organisation? To reflect your mission, your community, your voice?

source_text: BUILDING A PROJECT (10 min)
THE ORGANIZATION KNOWLEDGE BASE: Build a Claude Project for your organisation
Step 1. Start a new Claude Project and upload a document your team uses regularly.
(No document? Use your organisation's website copy, an old annual report, or your programme brochure.)
Step 2. Write a system prompt telling Claude to only answer questions using that document.
Example: "You are a helpful assistant for [org name]. Answer questions only using the materials provided. If the answer isn't in the document, say so."
Step 3. Try asking it:
"What does our organisation do?"
"Who are our beneficiaries?"
"What did we achieve last year?"
The goal:
Can a new volunteer or donor understand your work just by chatting with this assistant? Could your team use this instead of digging through folders?

source_text: Code - Transforming Data
Have Claude build your ideal data interface
Drag a data file you already use into the chat. (Beneficiary records, volunteer attendance, programme reach data, anything you track in a spreadsheet.)
Ask Claude to show you that data the way you actually want to see it:
How do you wish you could see this information?
Which numbers matter most to your team or funders?
How would you want to look at it (graphs, filters, sorting by district or programme)?
Example: "Can you look at our beneficiary data and make an interactive dashboard I can open in my browser, with a map by district and a breakdown by programme type?"
If you don't have data handy:
Use your organisation's publicly available annual report numbers, or ask Claude to generate sample data that looks like yours so you can test the interface first.

records.title: Build your ideal data interface
card: Drag a data file you already use into the chat — beneficiary records, volunteer attendance, programme reach, anything you track in a spreadsheet.

• How do you wish you could see this information?
• Which numbers matter most to your team or funders?
• How would you want to look at it — graphs, filters, sorting by district or programme?

The same Claude you know, now working directly inside your files, folders and tools.

source_text: Spectrum of Caution
Don’t use it
Use it in the browser (can’t access your computer)
YOLO!
Use it on your computer with low permissions (Don’t approve anything - just leave)
Use it on your computer with medium permissions (Review every approval)
Use it on your computer with lots of permissions (Approve most things)
Live dangerously!

records.title: Spectrum of Caution
records.intro: From most cautious to “live dangerously” — how much access you hand the tool:
bullets: Don’t use it at all.
Use it in the browser — it can’t access your computer.
On your computer, low permissions — don’t approve anything, just observe.
On your computer, medium permissions — review every approval.
On your computer, lots of permissions — approve most things. YOLO!

source_text: A combo!
Create a visualization of this list of scored candidates that I can run in my browser. Let me sort and filter them by all relevant fields.
ROLE AND GOAL: You are an expert technologist hiring manager. Your job is to help me assess the technologists in this spreadsheet.
STEPS:
Score each volunteer on their technology skills
Score each volunteer on how many roles they have around mentorship / advising

records.title: Help me sort these applications
card: ROLE & GOAL — You are an expert hiring manager helping me assess the technologists in this spreadsheet.
STEPS — Score each volunteer on their technology skills, and on how many mentorship / advising roles they hold.
records.question: Create a visualization of this scored list I can run in my browser — let me sort and filter by all relevant fields.
